LAW AND MOTION 302, SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT GUIDONI USA INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION. THE HEARING ON GUIDONI USA, INC.'S "MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION" IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 25, 2024 TO PERMIT JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY. ANY SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING SHALL BE FILED BY NOVEMBER 8. GUIDONI USA SAYS IT "HAS NO RELATIONSHIP AND NO MINIMUM CONTACTS WITH" CALIFORNIA. (MEMO. 3:12.) PLAINTIFF SAYS "GUIDONI HAS SHIPPED AT LEAST 34 CONTAINERS OF ITS ARTIFICIAL STONE PRODUCTS DIRECTLY INTO THE PORT OF LONG BEACH, AS RECENTLY AS OCTOBER 27, 2022." (OPP. 2:14-15.) JURISDICTIONAL DISCOVERY IS WARRANTED. (MAGNECOMP CORP. V. ATHENE CO. (1989) 209 CAL.APP.3D 526, 533. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay This Case Pending Silicosis Cases Petition For Coordination; Declaration Of Andrea A. Lowe
Off Calendar Jun-23-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Daniel Morse, Esq., of Brayton Purcell LLP. Tel: 415.898.1555 for plaintiff.
Anthony Ricucci, Esq., of Lydecker LLP. Tel: 415.706.3686 for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Caesarstone USA, Inc.

Hearing is held. The Court did not receive a contest email. According to counsel Anthony Ricucci, the email was sent to 'Contestdept302@sftc.org'; however, the correct email address for contest submissions is 'Contestdept302tr@sftc.org. The Court having heard oral argument simultaneously the 5 motions and having reviewed the pleadings, the Court adopts its tentative ruling:

1 - Defendant Caesarstone USA, Inc.'s motion to stay this case pending adjudication of the coordination petition is denied.

The statutes governing coordination vest the coordination judge with the power to stay the proceedings. CCP 404.5 provides that "Pending any determination of whether coordination is appropriate, the judge making that determination may stay any action being considered for, or affecting an action being considered for, coordination." Though section 404.5 does not deprive the court of the authority to stay proceedings, it evinces the Legislature's intent for the coordination judge to decide whether cases are stayed pre-coordination.

Order is Signed,

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom:
Scott Brust, Esq. of Metzger Law Group APLC, Tel. 562.437.4499, appearing for Plaintiff Jesus Aguilar Salmeron;
Alexandra Ascione, Esq. of Barnes & Thornburg LLP, Tel. 818.425.7337, appearing for Defendant Costco Wholesale Corporation;
Sadaf Nejat, Esq. of WFBM LLP, Tel.714.634.2522, appearing for Defendant Francini, Inc.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

The matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's motion to strike is denied as to the punitive damages allegations. As noted, plaintiff pleads a viable concealment claim that can support exemplary damages. The claim is alleged with sufficient particularity. (City of Sanger v. Superior Court (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 444 [punitive damages could be recovered in toxic tort case].) The court grants the motion to strike without leave to amend as to page 43, line 2 of the complaint ["and other silica-containing stone products to be identified during the course of discovery"]. The allegation is improperly vague, does not identify a product, and does not support a concealment claim under Jones v. ConocoPhillips Co. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1187, 1200 [plaintiff to identify product causing harm]. (See also Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 81 ["[t]he law cannot tolerate lawsuits by prospecting plaintiffs who sue multiple defendants on speculation that their products may have caused harm over time through exposure to toxins in them, and who thereafter try to learn through discovery whether their speculation was well-founded."].) If plaintiff discovers additional products from defendant that allegedly harmed him, then he can seek leave to amend.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order that tracks the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling verbatim.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom:
Scott Brust, Esq. of Metzger Law Group APLC, Tel. 562.437.4499, appearing for Plaintiff Jesus Aguilar Salmeron;
Alexandra Ascione, Esq. of Barnes & Thornburg LLP, Tel. 818.425.7337, appearing for Defendant Costco Wholesale Corporation;
Sadaf Nejat, Esq. of WFBM LLP, Tel.714.634.2522, appearing for Defendant Francini, Inc.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

The matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's fourth cause of action for concealment is overruled.

A claim for fraudulent concealment requires that: (1) the defendant concealed or suppressed a material fact; (2) the defendant was under a duty to disclose the fact to the plaintiff; (3) the defendant intentionally concealed or suppressed the fact with intent to defraud the plaintiff; (4) the plaintiff was unaware of the fact and would have acted otherwise if he had known; and (5) as a result, the plaintiff sustained damages. (Boschma v. Home Loan Center, Inc. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 230, 248.) Fraud generally must be plead with specificity. (Linear Technology Corp. v. Applied Materials, Inc. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 115, 132. But less specificity may be required where the facts lie more in the knowledge of the opposite party (as in this concealment case). (Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.)

There are "four circumstances in which nondisclosure or concealment may constitute actionable fraud: (1) when the defendant is in a fiduciary relationship with the plaintiff; (2) when the defendant had exclusive knowledge of material facts not known to the plaintiff; (3) when the defendant actively conceals a material fact from the plaintiff; and (4) when the defendant makes partial representations but also suppresses some material facts. [Citation.]" (Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651.) "Active concealment or suppression of facts by a nonfiduciary 'is the equivalent of a false representation, i.e., actual fraud.' [Citation.]" (Vega v. Jones, Day, Reavis & Pogue (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 282, 291.) As defendant's reply-not moving papers-note, the duty to disclose only arises if the parties are in a transactional relationship. (Bigler-Engler v. Breg, Inc. (2017) 7 Cal.App.5th 276, 311-312.)
Here, plaintiff's allegations are sufficient. Plaintiff identifies the silica-containing products that defendant sold and caused him harm. (Complaint, pgs. 42-43, pars. 111-117.) Plaintiff alleges a transactional relationship, active concealment, partial disclosure, and exclusive knowledge. (Complaint, pars. 1182-1186, 1194-1195.) Defendant implies that since it was a mere supplier-and not a manufacturer-the claim fails. But the extent of defendant's knowledge regarding the dangers of its products raises a factual issue. To that end, the distinction is not presently dispositive. (Jones v. ConocoPhillips Co. (2011) 198 Cal.App. 4th 1187 [concealment toxic tort case properly pleaded where defendant had notice of its product].)

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order that tracks the substantive portion of the court's tentative ruling verbatim.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Nguyen. (301/RCE)
